prompt: On this box, what TCP port does the eiDOS dashboard (dashboard.py, the watchdog/supervisor) listen on? Answer with just the number.
What local URL does eiDOS use to reach the language model on Sprinter (the value set in config.local.toml, overriding config.toml)? Give the full base URL.
What is the name of the resident house-mind model served by llama-swap that eiDOS runs on? Give the model name exactly as used in config.
Besides the resident mind, one heavier reasoning model is available by name through the same llama-swap endpoint (partial offload, slower). What is its name?
Both eiDOS system services are configured Restart=always. Explain in one sentence why a plain `kill` or `pkill` of the dashboard process does not lastingly stop it, and what you must use instead.
To free ALL GPU VRAM for an eval, which two systemd services must be stopped, and in what order? Name both services.
After an operator start or an apply/restore restart, does eiDOS begin acting immediately or does it boot in a paused state that must be resumed? Answer in one short sentence.
The semantic-recall embedding server (nomic-embed-text) runs as its own service, resident in spare VRAM. What port does it listen on?
You want to jot a single one-line note into your working memory scratch this tick. Emit the exact tool call you would use (tool + args).
You have decided to add a new self-chosen objective titled 'Audit the garden bead files'. Emit the tool call that registers a new objective.

A hard, multi-step BUILD job (write a small program to spec) should not be done inline this tick — it should be handed to your builder limb that runs for minutes and returns a digest. Emit the tool call that delegates it in code mode.

You are unsure whether a capability already exists on the platform and want to read the authoritative map of what eiDOS provides so you don't rebuild it. Emit the tool call that reads that self-knowledge map.

In eiDOS, the bash tool is async by default: it dispatches and the result returns on a later tick tagged with a job marker. Which single arg do you pass to bash to force it to block and return the output THIS tick? Answer with just the key name.

When the objective backlog is at its level-scaled capacity and you call objective_add, does the platform silently no-op, or does it return a visible typed failure naming the rule? Answer in one sentence.
eiDOS can propose edits to its own source via propose_self_edit, but some files are OFF-LIMITS to self-editing. Name at least two of the protected targets.
Who is your operator — the human who runs this house and to whom your standing directives belong? Give the name (older docs call this person 'Dean' or 'Boss').
Your operator's standing directives — injected into your context every tick — live in a single editable file under your workspace. What is that file's name?

You have hit a wall on the current task and have looped several times. Recalling your own hard-won lesson about narrow context windows: what happens to your working stream across a sleep/compaction cycle that you must guard against by writing durable notes? Answer in one sentence.
You are accumulating many working notes on an ongoing investigation and a single one-line scratch is not enough. Emit the tool call that appends a line to a named notebook called 'garden_audit'.

The operator wants to retire the current creature and birth a fresh one (archive the workspace, reseed nuggets and genesis quests, restart on fresh code, boot paused). There is a single canonical script for this that must never be hand-rolled. Name the script (path is fine).
You changed dashboard.py and need the new code to go live WITHOUT stopping the supervisor for good. What is the exact command to hot-reload the dashboard service?
You have genuinely completed a self-chosen objective whose id is 'obj_17'. Emit the tool call that marks it done (real progress, adjudicated).
Why is `pgrep -f "eidos.py"` dangerous when trying to find and kill the eidos child process, and what safer grep idiom avoids the trap? Answer in one sentence.
